//: Shape contract for the document ngx_test_probe_json() renders.
This file exists because field drift is only half-guarded. A rule that
NAMES a field already fails loudly when the probe stops emitting it
(assert.c: 'probe path "..." not present in document'). What nothing
catches is the other half: a field that is renamed, retyped or dropped
while NO current rule references it. That stays invisible until someone
writes a rule against it months later and gets a failure that reads like
a bug in their rule rather than in the probe.
So this is a schema for the EMITTER, not a validator for untrusted input.
The probe document is produced by our own code; the risk being managed is
'required' is per the variant a document belongs to. zone.present is the
discriminator: when the probe has no zone (or the zone's shm is not mapped
yet) the object carries present:false and nothing else is promised.
Module hooks may add members INSIDE the zone object via zone_render.
Those are deliberately not listed -- they belong to the consuming module,
not to this contract -- which is why unknown members under 'zone' are
allowed while unknown members at the top level are not.

notes.fds: -1 means /proc was unreadable, NOT zero descriptors. A delta over that sentinel cancels to a passing zero, which is why the prober rejects it explicitly rather than subtracting it.
notes.zone.present: false has two causes -- no zone configured at all, and a zone whose shm.addr is not mapped yet (a probe racing a reload). Neither promises the sibling members.
